intent: Extract per-section claims as three-layer claim arch (surface / buried / operational), plus rhetorical load, domain shift, and domain badges. Two-pass: claim-extractor.station does flat first-pass classification + claim block identification; LLM refinement produces the three-layer arch. Output feeds 06_CONTRADICTIONS, 09_GRAPH_LINKS, 10_RIGOR, 15_SECTION_PACKETS.

input.format: section-map.json supplies per-section text + section_id + heading_path + block_types. Lane 05 reads each section, runs the station's classifier as a first pass, then LLM-refines each block into surface/buried/operational triplets.
input.mock_policy: If 02 absent, lane runs the station's section splitter directly on the raw drop file. Mock provenance recorded in claim-packets.json under provenance.mocked = true.

output.format: claim-packets.json: page_id + sections[].claims[] where each claim is a ClaimArch (shared_lib/schemas.py:ClaimArch). claim-ledger.csv: Excel-ready flat. claims.md: human-readable. station-firstpass.json: raw output from claim-extractor.station/extract.py for traceability.

claim_arch_schema.buried_claim: the implicit claim that the surface claim presupposes
claim_arch_schema.operational_claim: what the surface claim commits the reader/system to do or measure
claim_arch_schema.domain_shift: true if the section crosses domains within itself
claim_arch_schema.domain_badges: subset of DomainBadge from shared_lib/schemas.py + AVIATION/SAFETY/PROCEDURE
claim_arch_schema.support_needed: true if claim requires evidence backing (feeds workflow tag 'needs-evidence')

loopback_conditions: A claim depends on a section span that no longer exists in 01_LOSSLESS (broken source span — write loopback artifact and skip the claim).
Station classifies 0 claims AND text length > 200 chars — likely upstream split failure.
LLM refinement produces buried_claim or operational_claim identical to surface_claim across all sections — prompt or input failure.
Math-bearing claim arrives WITHOUT corresponding 07_MATH_TRANSLATION entry — write loopback so math lane can revisit.

station_use.first_pass_output: claims_<run_id>.json with flat per-claim classification + signal-keyword scoring
station_use.override_output_dir: Run extract.py with --output-dir set to the workflow's 05_CLAIMS directory rather than the station's hardcoded D:\brain\08_CLAIMS\_OUTPUT. Round-1 captures station output as station-firstpass.json in this lane folder.
station_use.second_pass: run_prompt.md drives LLM refinement of each first-pass claim into ClaimArch shape.
